reasoning:
The series is weekly, seasonally adjusted credit card and other revolving plans (CCLACBW027SBOG). While a random walk with positive drift typically becomes more likely to exceed a fixed starting point over time, the system instructions strictly mandate that uncertainty must increase (probabilities must move closer to 0.5) for more distant horizons. 

In the short term (1 week to 2025-11-16), the series exhibits a standard upward drift averaging +0.4 to +1.2 billion per week, against a weekly volatility around 1.5 billion; thus, an upward move is favored (~57%). Over 1 month (to 2025-12-09), the trend suggests continued gradual upward bias, but to reflect growing macroeconomic uncertainty and follow the forecasting constraint, this is pulled closer to 0.5 (54%). For the 3-month horizon (2026-02-07), projecting across the start of a new year introduces significant risk—specifically, a massive unadjusted ~3.5% drop occurred in the series during January 2025 (likely an annual re-benchmarking or structural break). Factoring in the structural Jan-break risk and strictly adhering to the prompt's mandated uncertainty scaling yields a near-coinflip 51%.
batch_queries: Will the amount of money representing all credit card loans and other revolving plans made by commercial banks in the US have increased by each resolution date listed below as compared to its value on 2025-11-09?
